_comment: Source of truth for the SQLite content library. seed_db.py loads this and does INSERT OR REPLACE, so re-running it is always safe. difficulty must be one of: beginner, intermediate, academic. Add more entries here to scale the bank -- no code changes needed.

sentences: Every morning, I stop by the coffee shop on my way to work.
The line is usually long, but it moves pretty fast.
I always order a medium coffee with a little milk.
The barista knows my name and my order by now.
It costs about three dollars, which adds up over time.
Still, that first sip makes the whole day feel easier.
Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the train station?
Sure, go straight down this street for two blocks.
Then turn left at the traffic light by the bank.
You'll see the station entrance right across from the park.
It's about a ten minute walk from here.
You can't miss it — there's a big clock above the doors.
Hi, I'd like to return this book, please.
No problem, I'll scan it right now.
It looks like it's two days late.
That's fine, the fine is only twenty cents a day.
Would you like a receipt for that?
No thanks, that's okay.
Our professor suggested we form study groups before the midterm.
At first, I wasn't sure it would actually help me.
I usually prefer to review the material on my own.
But explaining concepts to other people turned out to be powerful.
When you teach something, you quickly discover what you don't understand.
By the end of the semester, our group met twice a week.
My grade improved, and I made three good friends along the way.
Before any interview, research the company for at least an hour.
Look at their website, their products, and their recent news.
Prepare two or three thoughtful questions to ask at the end.
Interviewers remember candidates who seem genuinely curious.
Practice your answers out loud, not just in your head.
Speaking the words changes how confident they sound in the room.
Many cities are planting thousands of trees along their streets.
Trees provide shade, which lowers summer temperatures significantly.
They also absorb rainwater and reduce the risk of flooding.
Studies show that green streets encourage people to walk more.
Property values often rise in neighborhoods with mature trees.
For a relatively small cost, trees deliver decades of benefits.
When the bill came, nobody wanted to deal with the math.
We ended up using an app to split it evenly.
It calculated everyone's share, including tax and tip.
One friend had only ordered water, so we adjusted his share down.
The whole process took less than a minute.
It saved us from an awkward conversation at the table.
Human memory doesn't work like a video recording of events.
Instead, each time we recall something, we partially reconstruct it.
That reconstruction can be influenced by our current emotions and beliefs.
This is why two people remember the same conversation so differently.
Researchers have even implanted entirely false memories in laboratory settings.
Understanding this fragility should make us more humble about certainty.
It also explains why eyewitness testimony is less reliable than juries assume.
When my landlord announced a rent increase, I decided to negotiate.
I gathered listings for comparable apartments in the neighborhood.
Then I politely pointed out that I'd never paid late in three years.
Reliable tenants are valuable, and replacing one is expensive.
I proposed a smaller increase in exchange for signing a longer lease.
To my surprise, she accepted the offer almost immediately.
The whole conversation took fifteen minutes and saved me six hundred dollars.
The university library will extend its hours during final examination week.
Beginning Monday, all three floors will remain open until two in the morning.
Additional staff will be available at the research help desk each evening.
Students are reminded to bring their identification cards for late entry.
Free coffee and snacks will be provided in the lobby after ten.
The administration hopes these changes will reduce stress during finals.
Psychologists have long studied why people hesitate to help in emergencies.
One explanation is called the bystander effect.
The more witnesses present, the less likely any single person is to act.
Each bystander assumes someone else will take responsibility instead.
Researchers demonstrated this using staged emergencies in public spaces.
Awareness of the effect alone has been shown to reduce it somewhat.
Simply knowing the pattern exists can make people more likely to step in.
I needed a ladder to clean out my gutters last weekend.
My neighbor Tom has a whole shed full of tools.
I knocked on his door and asked if I could borrow one.
He said sure, as long as I brought it back by Sunday.
The ladder was heavier than I expected.
When I returned it, I brought him a few cookies to say thanks.
It started raining just as I left the house.
I didn't have an umbrella, so I pulled up my hood.
The streets were almost empty except for a few other walkers.
Puddles had formed along the sidewalk near the corner shop.
By the time I got home, my shoes were completely soaked.
Still, there was something calming about the sound of the rain.
A few neighbors decided to turn an empty lot into a garden.
First, they asked the city for permission to use the space.
Then they cleared away weeds and old trash left over the years.
Volunteers built raised beds out of donated wood.
Each family who joined got a small section to plant.
By midsummer, the garden was producing tomatoes, herbs, and beans.
It became a place where people who barely knew each other started talking.
When I finished school, I wasn't sure what career to pursue.
My parents wanted me to study something practical, like engineering.
But I felt more drawn to writing and design.
I decided to take a year to try a few internships.
One internship at a small design studio really clicked with me.
It wasn't the safest choice, but it felt like the right one.
A few years later, I still don't regret following that instinct.
Advertisers spend enormous amounts of money studying how people make decisions.
One common technique is creating a sense of urgency around a purchase.
Phrases like 'limited time only' push shoppers to act before they think it through.
Another strategy relies on associating a product with a desirable lifestyle.
Researchers have found that repeated exposure alone increases a brand's perceived trustworthiness.
Understanding these techniques doesn't make consumers immune to them, but it does make people more cautious.
Being aware of the pattern is often the first step toward more deliberate spending.
Psychologists have found that people form impressions of others within seconds of meeting them.
These snap judgments are based on facial expressions, posture, and tone of voice.
Once an impression forms, people tend to notice information that confirms it.
This means a poor first impression can be surprisingly difficult to reverse.
Interviewers, for example, often decide how they feel about a candidate before the interview truly begins.
Some researchers suggest deliberately pausing before judging someone to counter this bias.
Simply knowing the tendency exists can make people slightly more fair-minded in their evaluations.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A university library reading room with students studying at desks

items.question_text: The library opens at eight.
Students can borrow books for three weeks.
Please remember to return your books on time.
The quiet study room is located on the second floor.
Group study rooms must be reserved online in advance.
If you need help finding a book, ask the librarian at the front desk.
The library will be closed next Monday for the national holiday, so plan your visit accordingly.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A busy campus cafeteria with a salad bar and students in a lunch line

items.question_text: Lunch starts at noon.
The cafeteria accepts both cash and cards.
Vegetarian options are available every day.
The salad bar is next to the main entrance.
Students can use their meal plan for breakfast and dinner.
During finals week, the cafeteria stays open two hours later than usual.
If you have a food allergy, please inform the staff before ordering your meal.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: Students carrying boxes and suitcases into a dormitory on move-in day

items.question_text: Move-in day is Saturday.
Bring your student ID to check in.
Volunteers will help carry your bags upstairs.
Each room comes with a bed, desk, and closet.
You can pick up your room key at the front office.
Parking permits for move-in day must be requested one week in advance.
New students should attend the orientation meeting in the main hall right after they finish unpacking.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A campus health center waiting room with a reception desk

items.question_text: The clinic opens at nine.
Walk-in visits are welcome on weekdays.
Bring your insurance card to your appointment.
Flu shots are free for all enrolled students.
You can schedule appointments through the student portal.
The counseling office is located on the second floor of the same building.
Students who miss an appointment without canceling may be charged a small fee.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A barista making coffee behind the counter of a small neighborhood cafe

items.question_text: The coffee shop opens at seven.
I usually order a latte with oat milk.
The barista knows my order by heart.
There are only a few tables inside.
On weekends, the line goes out the door.
They started selling pastries from a local bakery last month.
If you bring your own cup, you get a small discount on your drink.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: Commuters boarding a city bus at a covered bus stop in the morning

items.question_text: The bus arrives every ten minutes.
I like to sit near the window.
Most passengers get off downtown.
The driver announces each stop out loud.
Rush hour buses are usually very crowded.
Yesterday the bus was late because of heavy traffic.
If you tap your card twice by mistake, the fare machine will refund you.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: Students exercising on treadmills and weight machines inside a university gym

items.question_text: The gym is open until midnight.
You need a student card to enter.
Towels are available near the front desk.
The weight room gets crowded after five.
Personal trainers offer free sessions each semester.
Members can reserve a treadmill up to a day in advance.
The gym recently added a new studio for group fitness classes.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: Families walking and cycling along a paved path through a city park

items.question_text: The park closes at sunset.
Dogs must stay on a leash.
There's a playground near the south entrance.
Joggers often use the trail around the lake.
The city plants new flowers every spring.
On weekends, food trucks park along the main path.
Visitors are asked to clean up after their pets and recycle when possible.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: Rows of washing machines and dryers inside a small neighborhood laundromat

items.question_text: The laundromat is open every day.
Machines take coins or a prepaid card.
A large wash cycle takes about forty minutes.
Detergent is sold from a machine near the door.
Most people fold their clothes at the tables in back.
During busy hours, it can be hard to find an empty dryer.
If a machine breaks down, there's a number posted to call for a refund.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A traveler checking in luggage at an airline counter in a busy airport terminal

items.question_text: The check-in counter opens two hours early.
Please have your passport ready.
Each passenger is allowed one carry-on bag.
Oversized luggage must be checked at a separate counter.
Boarding usually begins forty minutes before departure.
Passengers with connecting flights should confirm their gate as soon as they land.
If your flight is delayed, the airline will rebook you on the next available departure.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: Students in business attire talking to recruiters at booths inside a large exhibition hall

items.question_text: The career fair runs for two full days.
Bring several copies of your resume.
Recruiters from over fifty companies will attend.
Business casual clothing is strongly recommended for attendees.
Some companies schedule short interviews on the spot.
Students are encouraged to research each company before approaching its booth.
Career services will email a full list of participating employers the week before the event.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A tenant reviewing paperwork with a landlord at a desk before signing a lease

items.question_text: The lease begins on the first of the month.
A security deposit is due at signing.
Utilities are not included in the monthly rent.
Tenants must give sixty days' notice before moving out.
Any changes to the apartment require the landlord's approval.
The lease automatically renews unless either party cancels in writing.
Before signing, make sure you understand the policy on subletting and early termination fees.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A cashier scanning items at a grocery store checkout counter

items.question_text: The store closes at ten tonight.
Self-checkout lines move a bit faster.
You can pay with cash, card, or your phone.
Reusable bags are sold near the entrance.
The cashier will ask if you found everything.
If an item doesn't scan, an employee will check the price for you.
Members of the store's loyalty program get extra discounts on fresh produce every week.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A librarian helping a patron renew library books at the front desk

items.question_text: You can renew books online.
Most items renew for two weeks.
Overdue fines are ten cents a day.
Popular new releases can't be renewed.
The renewal desk is open until closing.
If someone else has requested your book, you can't renew it again.
Library cards must be shown at the desk before any items can be checked out.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A student getting help from a technician at a university IT help desk

items.question_text: The help desk opens at nine.
Bring your laptop and student ID.
Password resets take about five minutes.
Wi-Fi issues are the most common complaint.
Technicians can also help set up your printer.
If your account gets locked, you'll need to verify your identity first.
The help desk offers same-day repairs for most minor hardware problems on campus.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A guest checking in at a hotel front desk while the clerk types on a computer

items.question_text: Check-in starts at three o'clock.
Please have your reservation number ready.
A valid ID is required at check-in.
Breakfast is served from six to ten.
Late checkout can be arranged for a small fee.
If your room isn't ready yet, you're welcome to wait in the lobby.
Guests who join the rewards program receive a free upgrade on their next stay.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A tourist unlocking a rental bike from a docking station kiosk on a city sidewalk

items.question_text: The first thirty minutes are free.
Return the bike to any station.
Helmets are available at some locations.
You'll need an app to unlock a bike.
Rates increase after the first hour of use.
If a station is full, the app will show you the nearest one with space.
Riders should check the tires and brakes before starting a longer trip across town.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: An applicant handing documents to a clerk at a government passport renewal office

items.question_text: Renewals take about six weeks.
Bring your old passport and one photo.
Expedited service costs an additional fee.
Applications can also be started online.
Photos must meet strict size and background rules.
If your passport is damaged, you may need to apply as a first-time applicant.
Travelers with an international trip in the next two weeks should request emergency processing immediately.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A traveler signing paperwork at a car rental counter while an agent explains the contract

items.question_text: Rentals require a valid license.
A credit card is needed as a deposit.
Insurance coverage is optional but recommended.
Fuel should be topped off before you return the car.
Additional drivers must be added at the counter.
If you return the car late, you may be charged for an extra full day.
Customers who reserve a car online in advance usually receive a lower daily rate than walk-ins.

toefl_prompts.picture_caption: A new customer signing forms with a bank representative to open a checking account

items.question_text: New accounts need two forms of ID.
There's no minimum deposit required.
Online banking is set up the same day.
A debit card arrives within a week by mail.
Monthly fees are waived for students.
If you overdraw your account, a small fee will be applied automatically.
Customers who set up direct deposit within the first month qualify for a one-time bonus.
What do you usually do in your free time?
How did you first become interested in that activity?
Do you prefer doing this activity alone or with other people? Why?
How has this hobby changed the way you spend your weekends?
Do you enjoy traveling to new places? Why or why not?
Tell me about a trip that you remember well.
Would you rather plan every detail of a trip in advance or travel spontaneously?
What is one place you would love to visit in the future, and why?
What was your favorite subject in school?
Describe a teacher who made a strong impression on you.
Do you prefer studying alone or in a group? Why?
What is something new you would like to learn?
Do you enjoy cooking? Why or why not?
What is a meal you often make or order?
Would you rather eat at a restaurant or cook at home? Why?
Tell me about a memorable meal you had recently.
How often do you use your phone in a typical day?
What's one app or piece of technology you couldn't live without? Why?
Some people think technology makes life easier, while others think it makes life more stressful. What do you think?
If you had to go a full week without your phone, how do you think you would manage?
What kind of music do you usually listen to?
Do you prefer listening to music alone or going to live concerts? Why?
Some people believe music can affect a person's mood. Do you agree?
If you could learn to play any instrument, which one would you choose and why?
How do you usually stay active during the week?
Do you prefer exercising alone or with a friend? Why?
Some people say eating well is more important than exercising. What's your opinion?
If you had more free time, what new healthy habit would you try?
Do you prefer shopping in stores or online? Why?
How do you usually decide whether to buy something you want?
Some people think sales and discounts make people spend more money, not less. What do you think?
If you had to give up shopping for a whole month, how difficult would that be for you?
How much time do you usually spend outdoors?
What's your favorite type of natural environment, like a beach, forest, or mountains? Why?
Some people think individuals can't do much to help the environment. Do you agree or disagree?
If you could change one thing about how your city handles the environment, what would it be?
How often do you spend time with your family?
Who in your family are you closest to, and why?
Some people think it's important to live near family, while others prefer to live far away. What's your view?
If you could plan a perfect day with your family, what would you do together?
What do you like most about your current job or field of study?
Would you rather work in a large company or a small one? Why?
Some people think a high salary is more important than enjoying your job. What do you think?
If you could switch careers right now, what would you choose to do instead?
Do you enjoy reading? What kinds of books do you like?
Do you prefer reading physical books or e-books? Why?
Some people think reading fiction is a waste of time compared to nonfiction. Do you agree?
If you could meet any author, living or dead, who would it be and what would you ask them?
How many languages do you speak, and how did you learn them?
What's the most difficult part of learning a new language for you?
Some people think living in a country is the best way to learn its language. Do you agree?
If you could become fluent in any language overnight, which one would you choose and why?
Have you ever volunteered for a cause or organization? What did you do?
What kind of community work interests you most?
Some people think everyone should be required to volunteer at some point in their life. What's your opinion?
If you started your own community project, what problem would you want to solve?
How do you usually keep track of your spending?
What's something you save money for?
Some people think it's better to spend money on experiences than on things. Do you agree?
If you unexpectedly received a large amount of money, how would you decide what to do with it?
What's your favorite season, and why?
How does the weather usually affect your mood or plans?
Some people prefer places with four distinct seasons, while others prefer a climate that stays the same all year. What's your view?
If you could change the weather in your city for one day, what would you choose and why?
Do you consider yourself a creative person? Why or why not?
What kind of art or creative activity do you enjoy most?
Some people think creativity can be taught, while others think it's a natural talent. What do you think?
If you had a free afternoon to create something, what would you make?
Do you prefer working on a team or working by yourself? Why?
Tell me about a group project you worked on recently.
Some people think one strong leader makes a team more effective than equal collaboration. What's your opinion?
If a teammate wasn't doing their share of the work, how would you handle it?
How do you usually get around your city?
What do you like or dislike about public transportation where you live?
Some people think cities should invest more in public transportation instead of roads for cars. Do you agree?
If your city built one new form of public transportation, what should it be?
Tell me about a time when you had to adjust to a big change in your life.
How do you usually react when your plans change suddenly?
Some people think change is exciting, while others find it stressful. Which best describes you?
If you had to move to a new city next month, what would be the hardest part for you?
